examples.title: Basic chdir and getcwd Usage
examples.description: Demonstrates changing directory and getting current directory with error handling
examples.explanation: This program first retrieves the current working directory using getcwd(), then attempts to change to /tmp using chdir(). It demonstrates proper error handling by checking return values and using errno to determine the specific cause of failure.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is path length

examples.title: Using fchdir System Call
examples.description: Demonstrates fchdir which uses file descriptor instead of pathname
examples.explanation: fchdir() is similar to chdir() but takes an open file descriptor to a directory instead of a pathname. This can be more efficient in certain scenarios, especially when the directory is already open. The O_DIRECTORY flag ensures we only open directories, not regular files.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for fchdir (no path resolution needed)

examples.title: Daemon Process with chdir
examples.description: Shows daemon calling chdir to root to allow filesystem unmount
examples.explanation: This daemon process demonstrates proper daemon initialization. The chdir("/") call is critical because if the daemon started in a directory on a mounted filesystem, that filesystem cannot be unmounted until the daemon exits. By changing to root (/), the daemon doesn't prevent any filesystem from being unmounted.

examples.title: chdir Path Resolution Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudo-code showing how kernel resolves path for chdir
examples.code:
FUNCTION chdir(pathname):
 // Step 1: Parse the pathname
 IF pathname starts with '/' THEN
 start_directory = root
 relative_path = pathname without leading '/'
 ELSE
 start_directory = current_process.CWD
 relative_path = pathname
 END IF
 
 // Step 2: Split into components
 components = split(relative_path, '/')
 
 // Step 3: Traverse each component
 current = start_directory
 FOR EACH component IN components:
 IF component == '.' OR component == '' THEN
 CONTINUE // Skip current directory or empty
 ELSE IF component == '..' THEN
 current = current.parent
 ELSE
 // Check if component exists and is directory
 IF NOT exists(current, component) THEN
 RETURN ERROR(ENOENT)
 END IF
 IF NOT is_directory(current, component) THEN
 RETURN ERROR(ENOTDIR)
 END IF
 IF NOT has_execute_permission(current_user, current, component) THEN
 RETURN ERROR(EACCES)
 END IF
 current = get_inode(current, component)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Step 4: Update process CWD
 current_process.CWD = current
 RETURN SUCCESS(0)
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how the kernel processes a chdir call: it parses the pathname to determine if it's absolute or relative, then traverses each component checking existence, type (must be directory), and permissions. Finally, it updates the process's CWD to the new directory inode.
examples.timeComplexity: O(d) where d is number of path components
